text:
ByKasey Nelson,

Former Contributor.

And that makes six. The U.S. added to their dominant Olympic all-around streak on Thursday with Simone Biles winning the Olympic all-around gold, her second time. Team USA has now taken gold for the past six Olympics. Biles scored a 59.131 inside Bercy Arena, Brazil’s Rebeca Andrade won the silver medal (57.932) and Suni Lee took the bronze (56.465).

This is the first time that two U.S. Olympic all-around champions went head to head in the all-around final. Lee won the title in the 2020 Tokyo Games and Biles in the 2016 Rio Games. On Tuesday in the team competition, Biles broke the record to become the most decorated American gymnast with eight medals, surpassing Shannon Miller, and now adds another gold for nine Olympic medals. She also became the first U.S. male or female gymnast to win Olympic all-around gold eight years apart.

The favorites going into the competition were Biles, Andrade and Lee, followed by Kaylia Nemour (Algeria), Alice D’Amato (Italy) and Manila Esposito of Italy.  These six athletes began on vault and rotated in Olympic order (vault, bars, beam and floor). As for scoring, each gymnast has a difficulty and execution score to determine the overall score on each of the six events. Each of those scores are added up for the all-around score.

Under the bright lights in Bercy Arena, everyone’s eyes turned to vault. Lee was up first, competing her Yurchenko double-full with a hop back to earn a 13.933. Andrade, who was expected to compete her new skill of a Yurchenko triple-full, played it safe with a sky-high and stuck Cheng (half onto the table front 1.5 twist off). This was a great start for Andrade who fired a 15.1 to put some pressure on Biles. Biles answered with her Biles II (Yurchenko double pike) that displayed huge height. Even with two steps back, she easily led the way on the event with her 6.4 difficulty and an overall score of 15.766. Andrade sat in second behind Biles by .666, and Ellie Black of Canada in third with a 14.066. Reigning all-around champion Lee was in seventh after one rotation, but vault is statistically her weakest event.





Andrade was up first on uneven bars and showed stunning lines with great rhythm throughout the routine and ending with a full-twisting double back dismount. She’s bringing the heat today with a 14.666 for her bar set. Biles mounted the bar next and does Tkatchev to Pac, catching the bar a bit too far and has to bend her legs to avoid hitting the mat. She finished the routine with a stuck double-double but scores a 13.733 and drops below Andrade by .267. Nemour surged to second ahead of Biles after her difficult bar set and hitting her double layout for a 15.533. Lee hit a beautiful set with an almost stuck full-twisting double back for a 14.866.

Heading into rotation three, this could be anyone’s meet with Andrade leading in first, Nemour second and Biles in third.





Biles was up first on beam, which is arguably the most nerve racking event. She performed a switch leap mount, switch leap to switch half to back pike and wobbled on her wolf turn. The front aerial split jump to straddle jump and triple series of back handspring layout to layout are no problem for her. She has a small wobble on her side aerial and understandably was looking a bit nervous but dismounts with a difficult back handspring to back handspring two-foot to full-in. This was a huge moment for her, and she fired a 14.566 to get back on track. Nemour has a big wobble on her back handspring to two foot layout but stays on the beam for a 13.033. Lee started off with a triple wolf turn, front aerial with a wobble to miss her connection out of it. She followed with a switch leap to switch half to ring jump, side aerial to layout layout and switch leap with a wobble, scoring a 14.0. Andrade mounted with a small wobble on her switch leap, switch leap to switch half to split jump and then a big wobble on her back handspring layout. She followed with a front aerial, ring jump to back handspring, switch ring, side aerial and finished with double pike dismount with a hop forward for a 14.133 to move her into second place.

Heading into the final rotation, Biles is in first, Andrade in second and Alice D’Amato jumps into third.





On floor, Lee started off with a stuck full-twisting double layout, 1.5 to front full and double back that comes up a little short, scoring a 13.666 to guarantee her a medal. Andrade was up next and started with a front full to full-in that went out of bounds followed by a full-twisting double layout, double layout and double pike. She fired a 14.033 to secure at least the silver. Biles' moment arrived, and she kicked her routine off with a Biles II (triple double), front full into a double-double, Biles (double layout with a half twist) and double layout for a 15.066 to secure gold.
title: Women’s Gymnastics 2024 Olympic All-Around Final Results: Simone Biles Is Golden
author: Kasey Nelson
description: And that makes six. The U.S. added to their dominant Olympic all-around streak on Thursday with Simone Biles winning the Olympic all-around gold, her second time.
keywords: Simone Biles Wins All-Around Gold,Suni Lee Wins All-Around Bronze,Rebeca Andrade Wins All-Around Silver,Women's Gymnastics 2024 Olympic All-Around Results,2024 Paris Olympic Gymnastics Results,Gymnastics 2024 Paris Olympics,All-Around Gymnastics,U.S. Women's Gymnastics 2024 Olympics
